response.justification: While the candidate brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and a FOSS4G talk, and has distributed-systems experience from their Sociomantic role, the core technical requirement of modern C++ or Rust is effectively absent. C++ appears only in a 6-month role from 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, and is not listed among current skills. The candidate's career has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js full-stack and AI-agent tooling, which is far outside the systems-programming domain this routing-pipeline role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and a FOSS4G 2019 conference talk demonstrates real familiarity with geo-data collection and mapping platforms
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed-systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, high-performance databases) shows relevant systems-thinking
Broad Linux background and demonstrated ability to work across multiple languages and paradigms suggests adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project; it is absent from the candidate's own skills list entirely
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
Current career trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, AI/agentic tooling) has moved steadily away from systems-level programming, making the gap to production C++ routing pipelines very large
